revision.text:
[[ja:胡麻手台16号墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Komatedai No. 16 Kofun | image = {{Image requested|cat=Sanmu City}} | affiliation = {{ill|Komatedai Kofun Group|ja|胡麻手台古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komatedai Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 胡麻手台16号墳|1=Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11611811}} | location = {{ill|Sanmu|en|Sanmu|qid=Q873528|lt=Sanmu, Chiba|1=Sanmu}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.64|N|140.398611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | area = Mound length 86 meters | built = Early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} | burial goods = Gilt-bronze ring fittings, straight swords, iron arrowheads, etc. | historic_site_status = Not Historic Site1 | burial = | map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Komatedai
No. 16 Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=38|coordinates=24.0|lon_deg=140|lon_min=23|lon_sec=55.0|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|24|N|140|23|55|E}}}} }} '''Komatedai No. 16 Kofun''' (胡麻手台16号墳, ''Komatedai Jūroku-gōfun'') is an {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sanmu|en|Sanmu|qid=Q873528|lt=Sanmu, Chiba|1=Sanmu}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Komatedai Kofun Group|ja|胡麻手台古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komatedai Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 胡麻手台16号墳|1=Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11611811}}. == Overview == The eastern bank of {{ill|Sakai River (Sanmu)|ja|境川 (山武市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai River (Sanmu)|1=Sakai River (Sanmu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11429014}}, a tributary of the {{ill|Sakuta River|ja|作田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakuta River|1=Sakuta River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11385396}} that flows through the {{ill|Kujūkuri Plain|ja|九十九里平野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūkuri Plain|1=Kujukuri Plain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q5120855}}, is home to numerous kofun groups, including the Haniya Kofun Group, Morokinai Kofun Group, Nezaki Kofun Group, Asō Shinden Kofun Group, and Shingyō-ji Kofun Group. Among them, Komatedai Kofun Group is considered a significant cluster, and Komatedai No. 16 Kofun is believed to be its central mound. It is a keyhole-shaped tumulus ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}) and was surveyed by the Chiba Prefectural designation1 Center in 1994. The survey revealed that the mound has a total length of 86 meters, a rear circular section with a diameter of 43 meters and a height of 7.8 meters, and a front section with a width of 49 meters and a height of 5.7 meters. A double moat system was identified. A {{ill|Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu (corridor-style stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki Sekishitsu (corridor-style stone chamber)|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} was found on the western side of the mound, but most of its upper part had been destroyed due to agricultural activities. The surviving section suggested a double-chambered structure with a total length of approximately 7 meters, a rear chamber length of about 2 meters, and a rear chamber width of about 0.6 meters. burial goods originally interred within the stone chamber were found in the surrounding moat. These included a gilt-bronze ring fitting, a straight sword ({{ill|Chokutō|en|Chokutō|qid=Q63821|lt=Chokutō|1=Chokutō}}), a decorative fitting, and iron arrowheads ({{ill|Tetsuzoku|ja|鉄鏃|1=Iron arrowheads|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tetsuzoku|13=User:Immanuelle/Iron arrowheads|qq=}}). The coffin nails and iron arrowheads were of a newer type than those typically found from the haniwa disappearance period. Additionally, no older-style elements were observed among the {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} burial goods. The "symmetrical double moat," a unitype-width moat encircling the mound, is also a more recent design. Alongside the {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae, Inba)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae, Inba)|1=Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11557461}} in {{ill|Sakae, Chiba|en|Sakae, Chiba|qid=Q1202754|lt=Sakae, Inba District|1=Sakae, Chiba}}, Komatedai No. 16 Kofun is considered one of the latest-stage keyhole tombs in Japan.{{Sfn|Hirose|Ōta|2010|pp=145}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hajime|authorlink={{ill|Ōtsuka Hajime|ja|大塚初重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Hajime|1=Hatsushige Ōtsuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11433969}}|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|authorlink2={{ill|Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|ja|小林三郎 (考古学者)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobayashi Saburō (archaeologist)|1=Saburō Kobayashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11461352}}|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten (Sequel to the Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|ja|続日本古墳大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten (Sequel to the Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|date=2002-09-01|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490105991|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|last=Hirose|first=Kazuo|authorlink={{ill|Hirose Kazuo|ja|広瀬和雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose Kazuo|1=Kazuo Hirose|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11485567}}|last2=Ōta|first2=Hiroyuki|title={{ill|The End of Zenpokoen-fun|ja|前方後円墳の終焉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The End of Zenpokoen-fun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|date=2010-10-01|publisher={{ill|Yūzankaku|ja|雄山閣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūzankaku|1=Yuzankaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11659206}}|isbn=9784639021568|ref=harv}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11505809}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/sanbu.html Shibayama Kofun & Haniwa Museum – Kofun of the Sakai River Basin] }} {{translated page|ja|胡麻手台16号墳|version=83477363|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:胡麻手台16号墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11611811}} [[Category:Image Requests for Sanmu City]]
